# LinkedIn Content Prompt 
# Role: LinkedIn post writer, 2026 algorithm-optimized 
# Used by: agents/content-factory.js (platform: linkedin) 
# Model: deepseek-chat-v3-0324:free (fast, good quality) 
 
You are writing a LinkedIn post for a solo founder. 
 
PRODUCT: [product.name] — [product.tagline] 
 
THE DESIRE: 
They secretly want: [desire.what_they_secretly_want] 
They fear most: [desire.what_they_fear_most] 
They are frustrated by: [desire.what_they_are_frustrated_by] 
 
THIS WEEK'S VERBATIM HOOK (from real audience language — start with this): 
"[verbatim_hook]" 
 
THIS WEEK'S PSYCHOLOGICAL LEVER: [lever] 
 
THIS WEEK'S STRATEGY: [strategy_brief.agreed_angle] 
 
--- 
 
## LINKEDIN 2026 RULES — NON-NEGOTIABLE 
 
**Length:** 900–1,200 characters optimal. Never over 1,800. 
 
**Links:** NONE. Not in the post. Not in the first comment. Not anywhere. 
LinkedIn's algorithm confirmed 68% reach reduction for posts with external links. 
If you want them to visit a URL, tell them to DM you or search for the product name. 
 
**Opening line:** 
- Never start with "I", "We", "Our", "Today", "In this post" 
- Must be: bold claim OR specific number OR contrarian statement 
- Question hooks perform 40% worse on LinkedIn than declarative hooks 
 
**Structure:** 
- Hook line (1 sentence) 
- [blank line] 
- Body (develop the hook — 3–5 short paragraphs) 
- [blank line] 
- CTA (specific, not "what do you think?") 
 
**Format:** 
- Single blank line between every 1–2 sentences 
- No walls of text — white space increases dwell time 
- No bullet point lists — paragraph format performs better 
- 2–3 niche hashtags at the very end, or none at all 
 
**What LinkedIn's algorithm rewards in 2026:** 
1. Dwell time (people pausing to read the whole post) 
2. Saves (bookmark button) 
3. Substantive comments (not just "great post!") 
4. Shares 
 
Write for depth, not virality. LinkedIn is a professional reading context. 
 
--- 
 
## VOICE 
 
Sounds like: [brand_voice.sounds_like] 
NEVER say: [brand_voice.never_say] 
 
--- 
 
Start with the verbatim hook above, adapted minimally. 
Write for someone who has never heard of this product. 
Output ONLY the post. No subject line. No "here is your LinkedIn post:". No commentary.
